title: Feature: hex-agent as Claude Code-independent runtime in Docker AI Sandbox microVMs

steps.description: Define port interfaces and domain types for sandbox lifecycle and agent runtime. Create ISandboxPort (spawn, stop, status, list), IAgentRuntimePort (execute_task, report_completion), and domain types: SandboxConfig { worktree_path, task_id, env_vars, network_allow }, AgentTask { task_id, description, model_hint }, ToolCall { name, args }, ToolResult { success, output, error }. Add to hex-core/src/ports/ and hex-core/src/domain/.
steps.done_condition: cargo check passes; ISandboxPort and IAgentRuntimePort compile with all methods defined

steps.description: Implement hex-agent MCP server with hex-aware file and git tools. In hex-agent/src/adapters/primary/mcp_server.rs, implement an MCP server exposing: hex_read_file, hex_write_file (with safePath + boundary validation), hex_edit_file (same guards), hex_bash (command allowlist), hex_git_commit (runs cargo check / bun test before commit), hex_git_status, hex_analyze (calls hex analyze and returns JSON). Wire the MCP server as a primary adapter. Boundary validation in hex_write_file must parse import statements and reject cross-adapter imports (S02, S03).
steps.done_condition: hex_write_file rejects paths outside /workspace; hex_write_file rejects cross-adapter imports; hex_git_commit runs cargo check before committing

steps.description: Implement agentic loop in hex-agent. In hex-agent/src/usecases/execute_task.rs, implement the loop: (1) receive task from HEXFLO_TASK env var, (2) score complexity via hex-nexus complexity module, (3) select model via inference gateway (POST /api/inference/complete), (4) send system prompt + task to LLM, (5) parse tool calls from LLM response, (6) dispatch to MCP server tools, (7) feed tool results back to LLM, (8) repeat until LLM signals done or max_iterations, (9) call POST /api/hexflo/tasks/{task_id} to mark complete. Escalation: after 3 failed test iterations, call escalate_tier and retry with next model tier (S09, S10).
steps.done_condition: hex-agent binary can receive a simple task via HEXFLO_TASK, call a local Ollama model, execute hex_write_file, and mark the task complete in SpacetimeDB

steps.description: Implement DockerSandboxAdapter in hex-nexus implementing ISandboxPort. In hex-nexus/src/adapters/secondary/docker_sandbox.rs, use the Docker Engine API (via bollard crate) to: spawn a microVM with the hex-agent:latest image, bind-mount the worktree to /workspace, inject env vars (SPACETIMEDB_HOST, SPACETIMEDB_TOKEN, HEX_AGENT_ID, HEXFLO_TASK, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY), apply network policy (allow list: SPACETIMEDB_HOST:3033, nexus:5555, inference endpoints). On macOS inject host.docker.internal; on Linux resolve host bridge IP. Return { container_id, agent_id }.
steps.done_condition: DockerSandboxAdapter::spawn launches a container, injects env vars, and returns container_id; container cannot reach hosts outside the allow list

steps.description: Add POST /api/agents/spawn route to hex-nexus. In hex-nexus/src/routes/agents.rs, implement the spawn endpoint: validate worktree path exists, resolve granted secrets from vault for the requesting session, generate a per-agent SpacetimeDB token (short-lived, scoped to agent identity), call DockerSandboxAdapter::spawn, register agent in SpacetimeDB agent-registry module, return { container_id, agent_id, status: 'starting' }. Add DELETE /api/agents/{agent_id} for teardown.
steps.done_condition: POST /api/agents/spawn returns { container_id, agent_id }; agent appears in hex agent list within 10 seconds of spawn

steps.description: Add SpacetimeDB WebSocket coordination to hex-agent startup. In hex-agent/src/adapters/secondary/spacetime_coordination.rs, on startup connect to SPACETIMEDB_HOST via WebSocket using SPACETIMEDB_TOKEN, call register_agent reducer, start heartbeat loop (every 30s), subscribe to swarm_task table filtered by HEX_AGENT_ID. On container exit (SIGTERM), call agent_disconnect reducer to cleanly de-register.
steps.done_condition: hex-agent running in a container appears as 'active' in hex agent list; after container exits, agent is marked stale within 45 seconds

steps.description: Add hex-agent/Dockerfile and Docker AI Sandbox configuration. Dockerfile: FROM debian:bookworm-slim, copy hex-agent binary, install Claude Code CLI (npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code), set WORKDIR /workspace. Docker AI Sandbox config (sandbox.yml): define network allow list, MCP Gateway integration, microVM resource limits (2 CPU, 4GB RAM). Add 'hex dev-rebuild' target that rebuilds the image after cargo build --release.
steps.done_condition: docker build -t hex-agent:latest succeeds; container starts and hex-agent binary runs; network policy blocks access to hosts outside the allow list

steps.description: Add worktree enforcement gate to hex hook subagent-start. In hex-agent/src/adapters/primary/cli.rs subagent-start handler: if HEXFLO_TASK is set in the input prompt AND cwd is the project root (not under hex-worktrees-*/), exit with error 'worktree_required'. Update hex swarm to default to Docker spawn path; add --no-sandbox flag to bypass for interactive Claude Code sessions.
steps.done_condition: Attempting to run a swarm agent in the project root with HEXFLO_TASK set produces 'worktree_required' error and the agent does not proceed

steps.description: Wire all components in hex-nexus composition root and write integration tests. Add DockerSandboxAdapter and spawn route to the hex-nexus router. Write hex-nexus/tests/agent_coordination.rs: (1) spawn two agents in parallel for different worktrees, (2) each agent writes a unique file to /workspace, (3) verify neither agent can read the other's file, (4) verify both agents appear in hex agent list with active heartbeats, (5) verify both tasks complete in SpacetimeDB. Also implement 'hex test coordination' in hex-cli/src/commands/test.rs.
steps.done_condition: hex test coordination passes: two parallel agents complete without filesystem collision; both appear in SpacetimeDB with heartbeats; hex test inference still passes 3/3

cargo.name: bollard
cargo.purpose: Docker Engine API client for container lifecycle management

cargo.name: spacetimedb-sdk
cargo.purpose: SpacetimeDB WebSocket client for hex-agent coordination (already used in hex-nexus)

mergeOrder: step-1 first (ports — all others depend on it)
step-4, step-6 parallel (tier 1 secondary adapters — both depend only on step-1)
step-2, step-5, step-8 parallel (tier 2 primary adapters — depend on step-1 + step-4)
step-3 after step-1, step-2 (tier 3 use case — needs MCP tools ready)
step-7 after step-2, step-3, step-6 (tier 4 Dockerfile — needs complete binary)

riskRegister.risk: Docker AI Sandboxes API changes — it is a new Docker feature and the API may not be stable
riskRegister.mitigation: Isolate Docker AI Sandbox specifics behind ISandboxPort; plain Docker containers can serve as fallback implementation
Detect OS at spawn time; inject host bridge IP (172.17.0.1) or use --add-host on Linux; abstract behind SPACETIMEDB_HOST env var

riskRegister.risk: SpacetimeDB SDK version mismatch between hex-nexus and hex-agent
riskRegister.mitigation: Pin spacetimedb-sdk to same version in both Cargo.toml files; add version check to hex test coordination

riskRegister.risk: LLM tool call parsing — different models format tool calls differently
riskRegister.mitigation: Use OpenAI-compatible tool call format (JSON function calls); hex-agent normalises model responses before dispatch

riskRegister.risk: Agentic loop infinite retry — LLM produces consistently failing code
riskRegister.mitigation: Hard cap of 10 iterations per task; escalation after 3 failures; task marked failed after cap with full trace in SpacetimeDB

successCriteria: hex test coordination passes: two parallel agents complete a coding task in isolated worktrees without any filesystem collision
hex agent list shows both agents as 'active' with heartbeats during execution
After containers exit, agents are marked stale in SpacetimeDB within 45 seconds
hex_write_file rejects paths outside /workspace and cross-adapter imports in all test cases
A simple coding task runs end-to-end through hex-agent using a local Ollama model — no Claude Code, no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY required
hex test inference still passes 3/3 (no regression)
